metadata.title: A Roll of Sourdough Armadillos
metadata.curiosityNote: Sourdough needs steady oven heat to puff, so losing baking time can leave rolls flat.

story.briefTemplate: For a school baking challenge, Grandma and Seraphina shaped dough into {quantity:shaped_armadillo_rolls}, complete with chocolate chip eyes. A power outage cut the baking time short, so {quantity:underpuffed_rolls} did not puff up enough to look like sourdough armadillos. When the power returned, Seraphina slid the tray back into the oven, and {quantity:rescued_rolls} finally puffed up enough to join the roll of other sourdough armadillos.
story.causalEvent: A power outage cut the baking time short.
story.closingNoteTemplate: The bake-off log closed with {quantity:final_presentable_rolls} presentable armadillos.

label.child: Rolls that did not puff enough
label.lowercase: the rolls that did not puff enough

label.child: Armadillos rescued in the second bake

steps.prompt: How many sourdough armadillos were ready to join the roll right after the power came back?
steps.reasoningPrompt: Some rolls did not puff up enough after the outage. Did the number of presentable armadillos go up or down?

backwardCheck.prompt: Add the flat rolls back in. Do you return to the shaped count?

steps.prompt: After the round in the oven, how many sourdough armadillos were presentable in total?
steps.reasoningPrompt: The initially presentable armadillos and the rescued armadillos both joined the roll. How do they make the total?

backwardCheck.prompt: Take the rescued armadillos back out. Do you land on the first presentable count?

operatorExperiments.alternateWorldTemplate: This matches the story: the flat rolls dropped out, so fewer armadillos were presentable.
Adding would fit a different story where {quantity:underpuffed_rolls} joined the tray instead of falling flat.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:underpuffed_rolls} groups like {label:shaped_armadillo_rolls.lowercase}, how many rolls would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:shaped_armadillo_rolls.lowercase} into equal groups.
This matches the story: {label:initially_presentable_rolls.lowercase} and {label:rescued_rolls.lowercase} combine into the presentable total.
Subtracting would fit a different story where {quantity:rescued_rolls} were pulled from the tray instead of added.
This would fit a different question: if there were {value:rescued_rolls} groups like {label:initially_presentable_rolls.lowercase}, how many rolls would there be?
This would fit a different question about sharing {label:initially_presentable_rolls.lowercase} into equal groups.

recap.headline: Why the bake-off log reached {quantity:final_presentable_rolls}
causalChain: The outage left {quantity:underpuffed_rolls} not puffed enough.
The second bake rescued {quantity:rescued_rolls}, making {quantity:final_presentable_rolls} presentable.

dataQuestion.prompt: What does {quantity:underpuffed_rolls} represent in the baking model?
dataQuestion.correctFeedback: Right. {quantity:underpuffed_rolls} is the number that did not puff enough after the power outage.
dataQuestion.incorrectFeedback: That amount is the number that did not puff enough after the power outage: {quantity:underpuffed_rolls}.
